steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: Sentence 1 says kyudo was not a medal sport. Sentence 2 says it was featured — but as a demonstration sport. The second sentence corrects or qualifies the first: it pushes back on the impression that kyudo had no presence at the Games. The transition must mark that contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Thus,': "Thus" signals a consequence or conclusion drawn from what came before. Being a demonstration sport is not a consequence of not being a medal sport. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Rather,': "Rather" means "on the contrary" and marks a correction or qualification of the previous statement — exactly what the second sentence does. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Additionally,': "Additionally" adds a point in the same direction. The second sentence does not simply add more of the same; it qualifies the first. ✗
  • Choice D — 'For example,': "For example" introduces an illustration of a general claim. The second sentence is not an example of kyudo not being a medal sport. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
